name: LIMB REDUCTION ANOMALIES
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF OBSTETRIC AND GYNECOLOGIC
definition: flexion contracture of PIP joint of any finger index finger overlaps clenched fist formed by other digits fixed abnormal deviation of 5th finger in radioulnar plane (= radial angulation of DIP permanently deviated axis of wrist + usually closed hand SPLIT / CLEFT HAND = LOBSTER CLAW HAND longitudinal deficiency of central digits overgrowth of all structures of affected (commonly radial) fingers missing / foreshortened arm / forearm with presence of hand presence of supernumerary digits association of polydactyly with syndactyly abnormal connection between adjacent digits same length of last 4 fingers sedative-hypnotic nonbarbiturate (sold 1954-1961)
dominant_finding: proximal interphalangeal articulation of index finger flexed + ulnarly deviated
discriminating_features: proximal interphalangeal articulation of index finger flexed + ulnarly deviated
deep V- or U-shaped central defect
hemimelia = absence / gross shortening of distal portion of one / more limbs (esp. radius

general: proximal interphalangeal articulation of index finger flexed + ulnarly deviated

hemimelia = absence / gross shortening of distal portion of one / more limbs (esp. radius
phocomelia = attachment of hands + feet to abbreviated arms and legs
amelia = congenital absence of one / more arms / legs
poorly developed diaphyses of long bones
hypoplastic terminal phalanges associated with dysplastic nails
